subtitle: Two hours either side of low tide, you dig your own bath in the sand over 64°C geothermal water — on one of New Zealand's deadliest beaches for rips.
spike_statement: Geothermal water as hot as 64°C seeps up through the sand from two fissures, so visitors dig their own spa pools near low tide.

levers.photo_tone: low wet sand at dusk, steam off dug pools, dark Coromandel headland behind, spades stuck upright
